Home / Tetra / TETRA vs Neo4j — Benchmarks, Pricing, Migration | $299/mo vs $1,051/mo

TETRA vs Neo4j

You’re paying too much for Neo4j.
And it still OOMs on writes.

Same queries. Same hardware. Same Bolt protocol. We gave Neo4j twice the RAM budget. Neo4j used 10.8× more memory. Crashed on concurrent writes. Failed half the LDBC Interactive Complex queries. Costs $1,051/month for 16 GB of RAM.

TETRA ran the full suite in 66 MB. Passed all 14 LDBC queries. Stayed flat at 64 concurrent clients. $299/month flat.

66 MB
vs 710 MB
RAM used
14/14
vs partial
LDBC IC queries
11/11
vs 0/11
Write operations
$299
vs $1,051
Monthly cost

Benchmark Results

We ran the benchmark. Here’s what happened.

105 Cypher queries. 30 iterations each. Both containerized. Neo4j got 1 GB RAM / 1 CPU; TETRA got 512 MB / 1 CPU. TETRA won 37, Neo4j won 19. TETRA completed all 11 writes; Neo4j OOM’d.

CategoryTETRA winsNeo4j winsTETRA speedup
Counts & Lookups501.1×–3.9×
Traversals52varied
Aggregation602.9×–63×
WITH Pipeline601.1×–22×
Real-World App701.4×–290×
Stress Test41varied
Total3719
Write ops11/110/11 OOM—

Throughput under load

Neo4j drops from 90 q/s to 29 q/s at 8 clients. At 64, crashes. TETRA: flat at 485 q/s.

Pricing Comparison

Neo4j’s real cost.

Published pricing for Neo4j AuraDB tiers. TETRA is flat-rate — visualization, algorithms, and full openCypher included.

ConfigurationMonthlyYear 1Year 5
AuraDB Pro 8 GB$526$6,307$31,536
AuraDB Pro 16 GB$1,051$12,614$63,072
AuraDB BC 8 GB$1,168$14,016$70,080
AuraDB BC 32 GB$4,672$56,064$280,320
TETRA$299$3,588$17,940

Neo4j add-on costs (not included above)

  • Bloom — $1,200–$2,500 / user / year
  • Graph Data Science (GDS) — $10K–$25K+ / year
  • Enterprise license — $20K–$200K+ / year

TETRA includes visualization, 30+ algorithms, and full openCypher at $299/month. No add-ons.

Migration

Migration in an afternoon.

100% Cypher TCK compliance — 1,611 / 1,611 scenarios passed. Same Bolt protocol. Point your existing Neo4j driver at TETRA’s Bolt endpoint.

1,611
Cypher TCK scenarios
100%
openCypher compliance
Bolt v4.4
Wire-compatible protocol

Transparency

Where Neo4j wins.

19 of 105 queries. Primarily multi-variable RETURN projections and OPTIONAL MATCH chains. These are known optimization targets on our roadmap.

Ready to stop overpaying?

Same Cypher. Same Bolt. Less RAM. Lower cost. See the full technical breakdown or schedule a migration call.
